differential_diagnoses: Septic shock (spontaneous bacterial peritonitis or translocation)
Acute viral myocarditis (e.g., Dengue or Chikungunya)
next_steps: Perform dengue NS1 antigen and IgM/IgG serology
Send peripheral blood smear for thick/thin films and quantitative PCR for Plasmodium
Obtain blood and ascites fluid cultures
Perform diagnostic paracentesis to rule out spontaneous bacterial peritonitis
Measure serum ferritin, triglycerides, and fibrinogen to evaluate for HLH
Continue hemodynamic support and escalate vasopressors as needed
Perform echocardiogram to monitor biventricular function and rule out pericarditis

differential_diagnoses: Septic shock secondary to spontaneous bacterial peritonitis
next_steps: Urgent diagnostic paracentesis and ascitic fluid analysis (cell count, differential, culture)
Infectious disease consultation specializing in tropical medicine
Peripheral blood smear for malaria parasites and Strongyloides larvae
Dengue NS1 antigen and IgM/IgG serology
Evaluation for HLH (serum ferritin, triglycerides, fibrinogen, soluble CD25)
Continued broad-spectrum antimicrobial coverage including anti-parasitic/anti-helminthic consideration if clinical suspicion for Strongyloides hyperinfection persists
Serial echocardiographic monitoring of myocardial function
